economic_notes: Nation is member of Ma-Mook Development Corporation (forestry operations, 5-nation organization). Ahousaht Fishing Corporation is a wholly-owned business entity accounted for by modified equity method. Mamook Natural Resources Ltd. held at 20% cost. Major capital projects underway: Wastewater Treatment Plant, roads and drainage improvements, Chimanah Wellness Centre, modular housing units.
notes: Audit is qualified for two reasons: (1) no approved budget was prepared for all operating segments, and (2) financial information for Ahousaht Fishing Corporation was not available at audit date, making it impossible to determine whether adjustments to investments, earnings, surplus, and accumulated surplus are required. A prior period restatement was recorded: opening 2022 surplus increased by $1,045,363 and deferred revenue decreased by $1,045,363. total_government_transfers estimated as ISC ($19,013,687) + Nuu-Chah-Nulth Tribal Council ($3,982,290) + First Nations Health Authority ($28,880) + Province of BC ($1,725,336) + Aboriginal Head Start ($222,943) + CMHC mortgage funds ($84,755) - however NTC and FNHA may not be direct government transfers; classified conservatively. osr calculated as total revenue minus ISC, Province of BC, FNHA, CMHC, and Aboriginal Head Start. No schedule of remuneration disclosed.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
